content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 21,
1994
Richard H. Miers,
C.P.A.
Miers
& Miers
135 Cal Avenue
P.O.
Box 609
Barstow,
CA 92312-0609
Your Request For Advice
Our File No.
1—94-256
Dear Mr.
Miers:
We write in response to your request for advice on behalf of
Jeffrey D.
Miers regarding application of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”)
1
OUESTION PRESENTED
Are any provisions of the Act violated by a certified public
accountant who audits city agencies when the accountant acts as
campaign treasurer and personal accountant for a city
councilmember?
CONCLUSION
The conduct outlined in your request does not create a
conflict—of-interest under the Act.
FACTS
Your partner, Jeffrey D.Miers has been asked by a present
member of the Barstow City Council to serve as his campaign
treasurer for the coming November election.
Your firm audits the
City of Barstow and its component units.
In addition,
you prepare
the personal tax returns of this councilinember and others.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


File No.
1—94—256
Page2
-
ANALYSIS
The Act does not prohibit Jeffrey Miers participation in a
reelection campaign as a committee treasurer.
It is not a
conflict-of-interest under the Act for public employees,
consultants,
or officials to participate as a treasurer or
chairman of a candidate’s committee.
As campaign treasurer,
however, Jeffrey Miers will be responsible for the record keeping
and disclosure functions of the councilineinber’s reelection
committee.
The Act’s requirements in this area are quite
extensive.
To aid your partner in his understanding of these
provisions we enclose copies of the campaign disclosure manual for
candidates and the 1994 supplement.
Should you have any questions regarding the technical
disclosure requirements imposed by the Act,
you may contact the
Commission’s Technical Assistance Division at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
7el4
By:
Daniel E.
Muallem
Counsel,
Legal Division
Enclosures

sections.conclusion:
The conduct outlined in your request does not create a
conflict—of-interest under the Act.

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission September 21, 1994 Richard H. Miers, C.P.A. Miers & Miers 135 Cal Avenue P.O. Box 609 Barstow, CA 92312-0609 Your Request For Advice Our File No. 1—94-256 Dear Mr. Miers: We write in response to your request for advice on behalf of Jeffrey D. Miers regarding application of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) 1 OUESTION PRESENTED Are any provisions of the Act violated by a certified public accountant who audits city agencies when the accountant acts as campaign treasurer and personal accountant for a city councilmember? CONCLUSION The conduct outlined in your request does not create a conflict—of-interest under the Act. FACTS Your partner, Jeffrey D.Miers has been asked by a present member of the Barstow City Council to serve as his campaign treasurer for the coming November election. Your firm audits the City of Barstow and its component units. In addition, you prepare the personal tax returns of this councilinember and others. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000—18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 File No. 1—94—256 Page2 - ANALYSIS The Act does not prohibit Jeffrey Miers participation in a reelection campaign as a committee treasurer. It is not a conflict-of-interest under the Act for public employees, consultants, or officials to participate as a treasurer or chairman of a candidate’s committee. As campaign treasurer, however, Jeffrey Miers will be responsible for the record keeping and disclosure functions of the councilineinber’s reelection committee. The Act’s requirements in this area are quite extensive. To aid your partner in his understanding of these provisions we enclose copies of the campaign disclosure manual for candidates and the 1994 supplement. Should you have any questions regarding the technical disclosure requirements imposed by the Act, you may contact the Commission’s Technical Assistance Division at (916) 322—5662. Sincerely, Steven G. Churchwell 7el4 By: Daniel E. Muallem Counsel, Legal Division Enclosures
